gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP oracle oldestObservation=0 fallback enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX silently degrades to spot price when oldestObservation < secondsAgo, including the degenerate case oldestObservation=0; amountOutMinimum is then spot*90%, making a single-block sandwich viable
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn, protocol receives fewer TORUS than fair value, attacker captures price impact profit

live_preconditions.name: oldestObservation == 0 (Case A: spot-price degenerate state)
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT MET — pool startTimeStamp=1752170400 (June 2025), liquidityAdded=true, over 1 year of activity; oldestObservation is almost certainly >= 900s

live_preconditions.name: TWAP manipulation profitable over 15-minute window (Case B)
live_preconditions.observed_value: UNKNOWN — pool liquidity depth not directly captured; large titanXUsedForBurns (4.2e30) and ethUsedForBurns (9.9e19) suggest active pool but do not confirm liquidity depth
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call <titanXTorusPool> 'liquidity()(uint128)' --rpc-url mainnet; cast call <titanXTorusPool> 'slot0()((uint160,int24,uint16,uint16,uint16,uint8,bool))' --rpc-url mainnet

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sets secondsAgo = min(900, oldestObservation); if oldestObservation=0 then secondsAgo=0 and OracleLibrary.consult returns current spot tick, not TWAP
amountOutMinimum = getTorusQuoteForTitanX(amountTitanX) * (100 - titanXToTorusSlippage) / 100; if oracle returns manipulated price, minimum is also manipulated
Unprivileged external entry point; calls _swapTitanXForTorus with interval-allocated TitanX; no additional slippage guard beyond amountOutMinimum

attacker_inputs: Timing of front-run/back-run transactions around swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
Case A: flash-loan TitanX to move spot price in single block (requires oldestObservation=0)
Case B: sustained large trades over <=15 minutes to shift TWAP by >10%
call_sequence: Case A only: [block N] attacker flash-loans TitanX, sells TORUS into pool to deflate TORUS spot price; secondsAgo collapses to 0; getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns deflated spot
Case B only: [blocks N-900s to N] attacker executes large trades to shift TWAP tick downward by >10%
[block N] attacker (or anyone) calls swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn; amountOutMinimum = deflatedQuote * 90%; swap executes at manipulated price receiving fewer TORUS
[block N] attacker back-runs: sells pre-positioned TORUS at restored price, capturing price impact
state_before: Case A: oldestObservation == 0 (NOT met by live state)
Case B: attacker has shifted TWAP by >10% (unverified profitability)
state_after: Protocol receives fewer TORUS than fair value for the TitanX spent
Attacker captures price impact profit from sandwich
totalTorusBurnt increases by less than fair-value amount

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX protocol funds converted to TORUS at below-fair-value rate; attacker profits from price impact
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Difference between fair-value TORUS price and manipulated price, minus manipulation cost
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol burns fewer TORUS per TitanX than fair value; deflationary mechanism is degraded
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is not entitled to any portion of the protocol's TitanX allocation; profit comes entirely from manipulating the oracle used to set amountOutMinimum

setup: Resolve torusToken.titanXTorusPool() to get TitanX/TORUS pool address
Call OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(pool) — if >= 900, Case A is rejected; proceed to Case B only
For Case B: use vm.warp + large trades over 15-minute window to shift TWAP tick by 10%+; verify manipulation cost vs expected profit at current pool liquidity
transaction_sequence: Case B: [t=0 to t=900s] attacker executes large TitanX buys to push TORUS price down, shifting TWAP
[t=900s] call swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn with attacker as msg.sender
[t=900s] attacker sells pre-positioned TORUS back at restored price
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If manipulation cost exceeds sandwich profit at current pool liquidity depth, downgrade to REJECT — TWAP provides adequate protection at current liquidity levels

rejection_reason: Case A (oldestObservation=0) precondition is NOT met by live state: pool has been active since June 2025 (startTimeStamp=1752170400) with over a year of continuous operation and large trading volumes, making oldestObservation=0 implausible. Case B (sustained TWAP manipulation) is theoretically possible given the 10% slippage tolerance, but profitability depends on pool liquidity depth which is not captured in live_context and must be verified empirically via fork test. live_state_supports_preconditions and concrete_fund_or_reward_gain cannot be set true without the fork test result.
